```python
expected_peaks = {"00000", "00010", "00100", "00110"}
peak_counts = {state: counts.get(state, 0) for state in expected_peaks}
total_peak_counts = sum(peak_counts.values())

# Verify expected peaks dominate the distribution (at least 70% of shots)
assert total_peak_counts >= 0.7 * shots, f"Expected peaks only account for {total_peak_counts}/{shots} shots"

# Verify distribution is balanced - no single peak should dominate
max_peak_count = max(peak_counts.values())
min_peak_count = min(peak_counts.values())
expected_per_peak = total_peak_counts / len(expected_peaks)

# Each peak should be within reasonable bounds of the average
assert max_peak_count <= 2.0 * expected_per_peak, f"Peak too high: {max_peak_count} vs expected ~{expected_per_peak}"
assert min_peak_count >= 0.3 * expected_per_peak, f"Peak too low: {min_peak_count} vs expected ~{expected_per_peak}"
```

```python
expected_peaks = {"00000", "00010", "00100", "00110"}

# Verify all expected peaks are present with reasonable counts
total_expected_counts = sum(counts.get(peak, 0) for peak in expected_peaks)
assert total_expected_counts >= 0.7 * shots, f"Expected peaks should dominate: {total_expected_counts}/{shots}"

# Verify distribution is balanced among the expected peaks
peak_counts = [counts.get(peak, 0) for peak in expected_peaks]
min_peak = min(peak_counts)
max_peak = max(peak_counts)
if max_peak > 0:
    balance_ratio = min_peak / max_peak
    assert balance_ratio >= 0.3, f"Peaks should be balanced, ratio: {balance_ratio}"

# Verify no single outcome dominates too heavily
max_single_count = max(counts.values()) if counts else 0
assert max_single_count <= 0.6 * shots, f"No single outcome should dominate: {max_single_count}/{shots}"
```

```python
expected_peaks = {"00000", "00010", "00100", "00110"}
peak_counts = {outcome: counts.get(outcome, 0) for outcome in expected_peaks}
total_peak_counts = sum(peak_counts.values())
non_peak_counts = sum(counts.get(outcome, 0) for outcome in counts if outcome not in expected_peaks)

# Verify that expected peaks dominate the distribution
assert total_peak_counts > 0.6 * shots, f"Expected peaks only account for {total_peak_counts}/{shots} counts"

# Verify distribution is reasonably balanced among the expected peaks
if total_peak_counts > 0:
    max_peak = max(peak_counts.values())
    min_peak = min(peak_counts.values())
    assert max_peak <= 3 * min_peak, f"Peak distribution too skewed: max={max_peak}, min={min_peak}"

# Verify non-peak outcomes don't dominate
assert non_peak_counts < 0.4 * shots, f"Non-peak outcomes too frequent: {non_peak_counts}/{shots}"
```

```python
expected_peaks = {"00000", "00010", "00100", "00110"}
peak_counts = {outcome: counts.get(outcome, 0) for outcome in expected_peaks}
total_peak_counts = sum(peak_counts.values())
non_peak_counts = sum(count for outcome, count in counts.items() if outcome not in expected_peaks)

# Verify that expected peaks dominate the distribution
assert total_peak_counts > non_peak_counts, f"Expected peaks should dominate: {total_peak_counts} vs {non_peak_counts}"

# Verify distribution is balanced among the peaks (no single peak > 60% of peak counts)
if total_peak_counts > 0:
    max_peak_count = max(peak_counts.values())
    assert max_peak_count <= 0.6 * total_peak_counts, f"Distribution too skewed: max peak {max_peak_count} vs total peaks {total_peak_counts}"

# Verify at least 3 of the 4 expected peaks appear with reasonable frequency
peaks_with_counts = sum(1 for count in peak_counts.values() if count >= 0.05 * shots)
assert peaks_with_counts >= 3, f"Too few peaks observed: only {peaks_with_counts} peaks with >=5% of shots"
```

```python
expected_peaks = {"00000", "00010", "00100", "00110"}
```
```python
observed_peaks = set(counts.keys())

# Verify we observe the expected peaks
assert expected_peaks.issubset(observed_peaks), f"Missing expected peaks: {expected_peaks - observed_peaks}"

# Check that the distribution is reasonably balanced among expected peaks
expected_peak_counts = {peak: counts.get(peak, 0) for peak in expected_peaks}
total_expected_counts = sum(expected_peak_counts.values())
min_expected_count = min(expected_peak_counts.values())
max_expected_count = max(expected_peak_counts.values())

# Verify expected peaks have reasonable coverage (at least 50% of total shots)
assert total_expected_counts >= shots * 0.5, f"Expected peaks only account for {total_expected_counts}/{shots} shots"

# Verify distribution isn't too skewed (max peak shouldn't dominate too heavily)
if total_expected_counts > 0:
    max_fraction = max_expected_count / total_expected_counts
    assert max_fraction <= 0.8, f"Distribution too skewed: peak with {max_expected_count} counts is {max_fraction:.2f} of expected total"
    
    # Verify no peak is completely absent if we have reasonable statistics
    if total_expected_counts >= 100:
        assert min_expected_count > 0, f"Expected peak completely absent in {total_expected_counts} relevant counts"
```

```python
expected_peaks = {"00000", "00010", "00100", "00110"}
peak_counts = [counts.get(peak, 0) for peak in expected_peaks]
total_peak_counts = sum(peak_counts)
non_peak_counts = sum(count for state, count in counts.items() if state not in expected_peaks)

# Verify that expected peaks dominate the distribution
assert total_peak_counts > 0.7 * shots, f"Expected peaks should contain >70% of shots, got {total_peak_counts/shots:.2%}"

# Verify distribution is balanced among the expected peaks (no single peak dominates)
if total_peak_counts > 0:
    max_peak_fraction = max(peak_counts) / total_peak_counts
    assert max_peak_fraction < 0.6, f"No single peak should dominate, max fraction: {max_peak_fraction:.2%}"
    
    # Verify at least 3 of the 4 expected peaks have meaningful counts
    significant_peaks = sum(1 for count in peak_counts if count > 0.05 * total_peak_counts)
    assert significant_peaks >= 3, f"At least 3 peaks should be significant, got {significant_peaks}"
```

```python
expected_peaks = {"00000", "00010", "00100", "00110"}

# Check that all expected peaks appear in the results
for peak in expected_peaks:
    assert peak in counts, f"Expected peak {peak} not found in measurement results"

# Check that the distribution is balanced - no single outcome dominates excessively
total_expected_count = sum(counts.get(peak, 0) for peak in expected_peaks)
assert total_expected_count > 0.5 * shots, "Expected peaks account for less than 50% of measurements"

# Verify no individual peak takes more than 60% of the expected peak counts
max_peak_count = max(counts.get(peak, 0) for peak in expected_peaks)
assert max_peak_count < 0.6 * total_expected_count, "Distribution is skewed - one peak dominates too heavily"

# Verify each expected peak has reasonable representation (at least 5% of expected total)
min_peak_count = min(counts.get(peak, 0) for peak in expected_peaks)
assert min_peak_count > 0.05 * total_expected_count, "Distribution is unbalanced - some peaks are too weak"
```

```python
expected_peaks = {"00000", "00010", "00100", "00110"}
observed_peaks = set(counts.keys())

# Verify we observe the expected period peaks
for peak in expected_peaks:
    assert peak in counts, f"Expected peak {peak} not found in measurement results"

# Verify the distribution is reasonably balanced across the expected peaks
total_expected_counts = sum(counts.get(peak, 0) for peak in expected_peaks)
min_expected_fraction = 0.15
max_expected_fraction = 0.40

for peak in expected_peaks:
    peak_fraction = counts.get(peak, 0) / total_expected_counts if total_expected_counts > 0 else 0
    assert peak_fraction >= min_expected_fraction, f"Peak {peak} has too low fraction: {peak_fraction:.3f}"
    assert peak_fraction <= max_expected_fraction, f"Peak {peak} has too high fraction: {peak_fraction:.3f}"

# Verify expected peaks dominate the distribution (at least 70% of all counts)
total_all_counts = sum(counts.values())
expected_dominance = total_expected_counts / total_all_counts if total_all_counts > 0 else 0
assert expected_dominance >= 0.7, f"Expected peaks only account for {expected_dominance:.3f} of total counts"
```

```python
expected_peaks = {"00000", "00010", "00100", "00110"}

# Check that all expected peaks are present with reasonable counts
for peak in expected_peaks:
    assert peak in counts, f"Expected peak {peak} not found in measurement results"
    assert counts[peak] > 0, f"Expected peak {peak} has zero counts"

# Check that the distribution is balanced - no single peak dominates
total_peak_counts = sum(counts.get(peak, 0) for peak in expected_peaks)
assert total_peak_counts > shots * 0.7, "Expected peaks account for less than 70% of total measurements"

# Verify no peak has more than 40% of total shots (balanced distribution)
max_peak_count = max(counts.get(peak, 0) for peak in expected_peaks)
assert max_peak_count < shots * 0.4, "Single peak dominates with more than 40% of measurements"

# Check that unexpected outcomes don't dominate
unexpected_count = sum(count for outcome, count in counts.items() if outcome not in expected_peaks)
assert unexpected_count < shots * 0.3, "Unexpected outcomes account for more than 30% of measurements"
```

```python
expected_peaks = {"00000", "00010", "00100", "00110"}
observed_peaks = set(counts.keys())

# Verify we observe the expected peaks
for peak in expected_peaks:
    assert peak in counts, f"Expected peak {peak} not found in measurement results"

min_peak_count = min(peak_counts)
max_peak_count = max(peak_counts)

if min_peak_count > 0:

# Verify that expected peaks dominate the total counts
total_expected_counts = sum(peak_counts)
assert total_expected_counts >= 0.7 * shots, f"Expected peaks only account for {total_expected_counts/shots:.2%} of measurements"
```

```python
expected_peaks = {"00000", "00010", "00100", "00110"}
observed_peaks = set(counts.keys())

# Verify we observe the expected peaks
for peak in expected_peaks:
    assert peak in counts, f"Expected peak {peak} not found in results"

peak_counts = [counts[peak] for peak in expected_peaks]
min_peak_count = min(peak_counts)
max_peak_count = max(peak_counts)

# No peak should be more than 3x another peak's count (balanced distribution)
assert max_peak_count <= 3 * min_peak_count, f"Distribution too skewed: min={min_peak_count}, max={max_peak_count}"

# Expected peaks should account for majority of total counts
total_expected_counts = sum(peak_counts)
assert total_expected_counts >= 0.7 * shots, f"Expected peaks only account for {total_expected_counts}/{shots} shots"
```

```python
# Verify the measurement distribution shows balanced period peaks
expected_peaks = {"00000", "00010", "00100", "00110"}
peak_counts = {state: counts.get(state, 0) for state in expected_peaks}
total_peak_counts = sum(peak_counts.values())

# Check that expected peaks dominate the distribution (at least 70% of shots)
assert total_peak_counts >= 0.7 * shots, f"Expected peaks only account for {total_peak_counts/shots:.2%} of shots"

# Check that each expected peak has reasonable representation (at least 5% of total peak counts)
min_peak_threshold = 0.05 * total_peak_counts
for state in expected_peaks:
    assert peak_counts[state] >= min_peak_threshold, f"Peak {state} has only {peak_counts[state]} counts, below threshold {min_peak_threshold:.1f}"

# Check balance: no single peak dominates more than 60% of the peak distribution
max_peak_count = max(peak_counts.values())
assert max_peak_count <= 0.6 * total_peak_counts, f"Peak distribution is skewed, max peak has {max_peak_count/total_peak_counts:.2%} of peak counts"
```

```python
expected_peaks = {"00000", "00010", "00100", "00110"}
observed_peaks = set(counts.keys())

# Verify we observe the expected peak states
peak_intersection = expected_peaks.intersection(observed_peaks)
assert len(peak_intersection) >= 3, f"Should observe at least 3 of 4 expected peaks, got {len(peak_intersection)}"

# Check distribution balance - no single peak should dominate
peak_counts = [counts.get(peak, 0) for peak in expected_peaks]
total_peak_counts = sum(peak_counts)
max_peak_count = max(peak_counts)

# No peak should have more than 60% of total peak probability
max_peak_fraction = max_peak_count / total_peak_counts if total_peak_counts > 0 else 0
assert max_peak_fraction <= 0.6, f"Peak distribution too skewed: max peak has {max_peak_fraction:.2f} of total"

# Expected peaks should account for majority of measurements
peak_fraction = total_peak_counts / shots
assert peak_fraction >= 0.7, f"Expected peaks only account for {peak_fraction:.2f} of measurements"
```

```python
expected_peaks = {"00000", "00010", "00100", "00110"}
```
```python
peak_counts = {state: counts.get(state, 0) for state in expected_peaks}
total_peak_counts = sum(peak_counts.values())
non_peak_counts = sum(count for state, count in counts.items() if state not in expected_peaks)

# Verify that expected peaks dominate the distribution
assert total_peak_counts > 0.6 * shots, f"Expected peaks should account for >60% of outcomes, got {total_peak_counts/shots:.2%}"

# Verify peaks are reasonably balanced (no single peak dominates)
max_peak_count = max(peak_counts.values())
min_peak_count = min(count for count in peak_counts.values() if count > 0)
if min_peak_count > 0:
    balance_ratio = max_peak_count / min_peak_count
    assert balance_ratio < 5.0, f"Peak distribution too imbalanced: max/min ratio = {balance_ratio:.1f}"

# Verify we don't have excessive noise in non-peak states
assert non_peak_counts < 0.4 * shots, f"Non-peak states should be <40% of outcomes, got {non_peak_counts/shots:.2%}"
```
